# Code Review ‚Äî n4 Codex session inheritance

## CRITICAL

### R1 ‚Äî `open-ready` loses the fresh parent-session proof and emits unusable Codex cards

- Production seam: `scripts/kaola-workflow-next-action.js:110-118` attaches the fresh proof to each
  ready descriptor, and `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js:1362-1379` consumes that descriptor
  proof when building a card.
- Defect: the running-set scheduler maps `toOpen` into `newNodes` at
  `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js:6502-6528` but retains only the tier/model metadata, not
  `codex_session_proof`. It later constructs each card from that reduced object at
  `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js:6840-6853`, again passing neither the proof nor a Codex
  runtime/session-proof context.
- Reproduction: a read-only direct harness passed two `open-ready` descriptors carrying the same
  fresh bound proof (`gpt-5.6-sol/xhigh`). Both returned dispatch cards contained
  `codex_model: null`, `codex_reasoning_effort: null`, and no `codex_session_proof_status`:

  ```json
  [
    {"id":"a","codex_model":null,"codex_reasoning_effort":null},
    {"id":"b","codex_model":null,"codex_reasoning_effort":null}
  ]
  ```

- Impact: the default running-set path mutates the nodes to `in_progress` but cannot satisfy the
  inherited-card/runtime-proof contract before spawn. This affects read fan-out and any serial/write
  node opened through `open-ready`; generated GitLab/Gitea ports mirror the same defect. The serial
  `open-next` and fused-advance paths retain the original next-action descriptor and do not reproduce it.
- Missing regression: the existing `open-ready` persistence case at
  `scripts/test-adaptive-node.js:5848-5878` stubs and asserts only `model`; it never supplies or asserts
  `codex_session_proof`, `codex_model`, `codex_reasoning_effort`, or proof status. This explains why
  n4's 2,166-assertion adaptive-node run remained green.
- Required repair: carry the corresponding fresh `toOpen` proof into immediate dispatch-card
  construction without treating it as durable across later parent turns, add open-ready regression
  coverage for fresh/absent proof, regenerate both aggregator families, and rerun the focused plus
  four-edition gates.

## HIGH

None.

## MEDIUM

### R2 ‚Äî Session-proof discovery is unbounded and materializes every session file

`scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:58-75` recursively enumerates every `.jsonl` under
`CODEX_HOME/sessions`, synchronously reads each complete file, splits every line, and parses every
event before checking the requested thread. Large/deep session histories can exhaust wall time,
memory, or stack, and unrelated prompt/tool events are unnecessarily materialized. The three plugin
resolver mirrors are byte-identical. Bound discovery by candidate file count/depth/size, identify the
thread from minimal metadata, and parse only the unique candidate needed for proof.

### R3 ‚Äî One unrelated malformed session file denies a valid current-session proof

The catch at `scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:71-75` returns `absent()` for the whole
scan before checking whether the unreadable/malformed JSONL belongs to the requested thread. The
result is consumed by `scripts/kaola-workflow-next-action.js:310-314`; therefore any stale truncated
rollout anywhere in the sessions tree can permanently block Codex reasoning-floor dispatch even when
the current rollout is valid. Skip invalid unrelated candidates and fail closed only when the uniquely
bound current-session rollout is malformed or ambiguous. Add a valid-current-plus-invalid-unrelated
fixture; current loader tests cover only one fully valid file (`scripts/test-agent-model-resolver.js:155-169`).

## LOW

### R4 ‚Äî Next-action exposes more proof correlation metadata than dispatch needs

`scripts/kaola-workflow-next-action.js:110-118` copies the full proof, including `thread_id` and
`observed_at`, onto every ready descriptor and the CLI serializes it at line 315. Keep binding fields
internal where practical and emit the minimum verified status/model/effort payload downstream.

### R5 ‚Äî Runtime comments still document the retired pinned contract

Several touched runtime families now implement inheritance but retain comments asserting Sol/medium
and Sol/xhigh role pins or `codex_profile_tier_mismatch`, including
`scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js:43-46,85-89,123-135`,
`scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js:1363-1365`,
`scripts/kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js:7-13`,
`scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:12-15`, and
`plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/install-codex-agent-profiles.js:107-110,204-209` (plus mirrors).
They are not executable pins, but they now contradict the implementation and should be updated during
the repair cycle.

## Confirmed contracts

- The n2-proved inheritance representation is used: omission of both top-level runtime keys. A
  mechanical audit found 48 profiles, 0 `model`/`model_reasoning_effort` lines, and 0 same-role triple
  drift; `scripts/test-agent-profile-parity.js` passed 211 assertions.
- Installer/preflight classification recognizes omission as inherited, exact historical Sol/medium or
  Sol/xhigh as `legacy_pinned`, and other/partial pins as malformed
  (`plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/install-codex-agent-profiles.js:245-300`,
  `scripts/kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js:793-843`). Legacy pins participate in stale detection,
  autofix re-verification, doctor reporting, and global freshness at
  `scripts/kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js:1347-1429,1596-1603,1661-1695`.
- The migration test passes and proves the project root config remains byte-identical across profile
  repair (`scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js:450-475`). No root config file is part of the n4 diff.
- Tier tokens and legacy aliases remain metadata (`scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js:49-65`),
  wait budgets remain reasoning=40/standard=20 at lines 163-183, Claude mapping remains at lines 68-75,
  and opencode mapping remains at lines 288-304. Focused resolver, next-action, handoff, installer,
  routing, and profile-parity tests passed.
- The Codex floor is fail-closed at `scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:97-132` and is called
  on the dispatchable frontier at `scripts/kaola-workflow-next-action.js:150-164`: absent, stale, and
  sub-floor proof refuse; fresh bound Sol/xhigh passes. Inheritance alone does not satisfy the floor.
- Routing generation and edition synchronization are mechanically clean: all 12 routing surfaces
  match the skeleton, all generated aggregator headers are present, and 10 forge aggregator ports,
  24 COMMON_SCRIPTS mirrors, and 27 byte-identical groups match their canonical source.
- The routing skeleton and three generated Codex plan-run SKILLs require one parent-equals-child proof,
  named base role identity, installed-profile binding, `fork_turns: "none"`, and omitted transient
  strength overrides. The three adapt SKILLs retain neutral tier/alias language.
- The standalone forge-neutral checks passed over all 19 GitLab and all 19 Gitea profile/command/SKILL
  prose files. No surviving executable per-role Codex strength pin was found.

## Security review

A delegated read-only filesystem-risk pass found R2-R4 above. It found no thread-ID path traversal
(the ID is not joined into a path), nested symlinks are ignored by `Dirent` classification, and no new
installer/preflight security regression was found. An environment-selected `CODEX_HOME/sessions` root
symlink is followed, which reinforces the need for bounded discovery.

## Validation evidence

- Upstream RED/GREEN evidence read in full from
  `kaola-workflow/issue-687/.cache/n4-inherit-runtime-and-profiles.md`; it records all focused checks and
  all four edition chains green.
- `node scripts/test-agent-profile-parity.js` ‚Äî pass (211 assertions).
- `node scripts/test-agent-model-resolver.js` ‚Äî pass.
- `node scripts/test-next-action.js` ‚Äî pass (121 assertions).
- `node scripts/test-adaptive-handoff.js` ‚Äî pass (153 assertions).
- `node scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js` ‚Äî pass.
- `node scripts/test-route-reachability.js` ‚Äî pass (575 assertions).
- Root, GitLab, and Gitea contract validators ‚Äî pass.
- `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` ‚Äî pass.
- `node scripts/generate-routing-surfaces.js --check` ‚Äî pass.
- Standalone GitLab `--forbidden-only` over 19 files ‚Äî pass.
- Standalone Gitea `--forbidden-only` over 19 files ‚Äî pass.
- Mechanical profile audit ‚Äî `{profiles:48, roles:16, pin_lines:0, triple_drift:0}`.
- `git diff --check` ‚Äî pass.
- Direct `runOpenReady` proof-retention harness ‚Äî fail as reproduced in R1.

## Verdict

BLOCK. One CRITICAL dispatch-path regression and two in-scope MEDIUM filesystem/proof-loader defects
remain open. No Finalization or live inheritance acceptance gate should proceed until the repair is
implemented and its regression tests are green.

# Findings

## CRITICAL

None.

## HIGH

None.

## MEDIUM

### R6 ‚Äî Session proof validation and consumption use different file identities

`scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:87-111` validates the discovered path with
`statSync`, reads its prefix through one descriptor, closes that descriptor, and later reopens the
pathname with `readFileSync`. The byte-identical plugin mirrors have the same defect at
`plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:87-111`,
`plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:87-111`, and
`plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:87-111`.

A writer able to modify `CODEX_HOME/sessions` can replace the uniquely bound regular file after
discovery with a symlink, FIFO, or oversized file. The delegated security review's read-only race
harness replaced a discovered `high` rollout with a symlink to an `xhigh` rollout; the loader
returned `fresh gpt-5.6-sol/xhigh`. This bypasses both proof identity and the 16 MiB size cap, so the
result can forge the reasoning-floor input or block/unbound the second read. Open and validate a
regular file with no-follow/nonblocking semantics, retain that descriptor, verify descriptor
identity/size with `fstat`, and perform the bounded full parse through that same descriptor. Add a
swap/race regression.

### R7 ‚Äî Exhausting a discovery bound does not invalidate the uniqueness claim

`scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:69-109` stops at the file/directory/entry limits, but
does not record that traversal was incomplete. It can therefore return `fresh` for one candidate
even though unvisited files could contain another rollout bound to the same thread id. The same code
is present in the three plugin resolver mirrors at lines 69-109.

A read-only repository test created 2,049 JSONL files with `CODEX_SESSION_SCAN_MAX_FILES === 2048`,
placed one valid requested candidate among the scanned files, and observed `result:"fresh"` after
the file limit truncated discovery. This contradicts the exact-one/ambiguous-fails-closed contract.
Track whether any bound terminated discovery before exhaustion of the directory tree and return
`absent` whenever uniqueness could not be established. Add limit-exhaustion tests, including a
matching duplicate beyond the scanned frontier.

## LOW

### R8 ‚Äî A legacy pair suppresses unrelated malformed-profile diagnostics

`scripts/kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js:1061-1064` puts every profile classified
`legacy_pinned` into `legacyPinnedProfiles` without considering other errors returned by
`validateProfileText`. The classifier at `scripts/kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js:830-842` examines
only model/effort lines, so a profile with the exact old pair plus a wrong role name, missing identity,
or invalid instructions is reported as safely migratable `profiles_stale` rather than
`profiles_malformed`. The byte-identical plugin preflight mirrors carry the same logic.

The review reproduced this with an `implementer.toml` containing the old Sol/medium pair and
`name = "wrong-role"`: `inspectScope` returned `malformed:[]` and listed the file only under
`legacyPinnedProfiles`. Classify a legacy pin as stale only when the remaining schema is valid (or
filter only the two expected omission reasons); preserve all other validation errors as malformed.
Add mixed legacy-pin-plus-invalid-metadata coverage.

### R9 ‚Äî Retired static-profile comments still describe the old runtime contract

Several changed assertion surfaces still tell maintainers that role profiles own static
Sol/medium or Sol/xhigh pairs even though their executable assertions now require inheritance:

- `scripts/test-agent-model-resolver.js:25-26` says no role default may produce a null Codex pair,
  immediately before assertions at lines 38-42 require null/inherited values.
- `scripts/test-route-reachability.js:213-217` and line 313 describe pinned/static pair classes.
- `plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-gitlab-contracts.js:908`,
  lines 962-1006, and the Gitea twin at lines 913 and 967-1006 retain the retired profile-pin schema
  wording and assertion messages.
- `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-schema.js:71` and its mirrors still call absent-tier handling
  `role-static resolution`.

These comments are non-executable, but they directly contradict the new maintenance contract and
leave prior R5 only partially resolved. Rewrite them as declarative tier/default metadata and
historical migration-pair checks; retain the historical pair constants where the migration
classifier needs them.

# Prior finding recheck

- R1 resolved: `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js:6501-6504` retains proof only in a transient
  lookup, lines 6842-6858 feed it to the immediate card, and the 2,169-assertion adaptive-node suite
  verified fresh and absent cards while keeping proof out of `running-set.json`.
- R2 partially resolved: traversal, prefix, and full-file reads now have explicit bounds at
  `scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:51-56,69-109`; R6 and R7 are the remaining
  validation/use and incomplete-scan failures in that repair.
- R3 resolved: unrelated malformed prefixes are skipped at
  `scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:93-103`, while uniquely bound malformed content
  fails absent at lines 115-125. `test-agent-model-resolver.js` passed.
- R4 resolved: `scripts/kaola-workflow-next-action.js:49-53,115-123` serializes only
  status/model/reasoning_effort; thread id, timestamp, and source do not enter node descriptors.
- R5 partially resolved: primary production comments were updated, but R9 lists contradictory
  comments left in the accumulated changed surfaces.

# Confirmed contracts

- All 48 role profiles omit executable `model` and `model_reasoning_effort` keys; the mechanical
  audit found 16 roles, zero pins, and zero cross-forge profile drift.
- `codexProfilePolicy` now treats every known role as inheritance-compatible, while neutral tiers
  and legacy aliases remain metadata. The focused resolver, next-action, adaptive-node, and handoff
  suites passed, including fail-closed missing/stale/sub-floor reasoning proof cases.
- Legacy full-pin migration removes role-profile keys without rewriting the root-level user-owned
  dispatch posture; the installer rendering/config/manifest/upgrade tests passed.
- Claude/opencode controls, wait-budget behavior, routing generation, and edition mirrors remained
  green in the focused tests and upstream four-edition chains.
- Scope matches the frozen n4 write set: 91 tracked files, with no public docs, changelog, opencode
  config, or unrelated product surfaces changed. The issue workflow directory is expected cache/state.

# Validation

Reviewer reruns:

- `node scripts/test-agent-model-resolver.js` ‚Äî pass.
- `node scripts/test-next-action.js` ‚Äî pass, 122 assertions.
- `node scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` ‚Äî pass, 2,169 assertions.
- `node scripts/test-adaptive-handoff.js` ‚Äî pass, 153 assertions.
- `node scripts/test-agent-profile-parity.js` ‚Äî pass, 211 assertions.
- `node scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js` ‚Äî pass.
- `node scripts/test-install-adaptive-config.js` ‚Äî pass.

- `node scripts/test-install-upgrade-rewrite.js` ‚Äî pass.
- `node scripts/test-route-reachability.js` ‚Äî pass, 575 assertions.
- Root, GitLab, and Gitea contract validators ‚Äî pass.
- Exact GitLab and Gitea `--forbidden-only` checks over all 19 profile/command/SKILL inputs per
  edition ‚Äî pass.
- `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` ‚Äî pass: 10 forge aggregator ports, 24 common-script mirrors,
  and 27 byte-identical groups.
- `node scripts/generate-routing-surfaces.js --check` ‚Äî pass: all 12 surfaces match the skeleton.
- `git diff --check` ‚Äî pass.

Upstream n4 additionally records sequential passes for
`npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude`, `npm run test:kaola-workflow:codex`,
`npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitlab`, and `npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitea`.

# Security review

The required delegated security recheck completed. It confirmed the privacy minimization and
malformed-unrelated behavior, but reproduced R6's path-reopen race. No additional privacy leak was
found.

WARNING. There are no CRITICAL findings, so the prose verdict maps to `verdict: pass` and

# Findings

## CRITICAL

None.

## HIGH

None.

## MEDIUM

### R10 ‚Äî Equal-size in-place mutation is invisible to the descriptor stability check

`scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:63-65` defines descriptor stability as equal
device, inode, regular-file type, and size. The checks at lines 169-173 therefore detect path
replacement, growth, shrinkage, and file-type substitution, but not an equal-length rewrite of the
already-open inode after prefix classification. The byte-identical resolver mirrors under
`plugins/kaola-workflow{,-gitlab,-gitea}/scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js` have the same
logic and line locations.

The review independently reproduced the race with a same-size compensated JSONL record: the prefix
read classified a `high` parent turn, the same inode was rewritten without changing its length, and
the full read returned `status:"fresh", reasoning_effort:"xhigh"`. This is a local correctness and
fail-closed validation defect because the proof consumed is not the version that was classified.

Use `fs.fstatSync(fd, { bigint: true })` (or an equivalently precise interface) for the retained
descriptor and compare high-resolution `ctimeNs` and `mtimeNs`, in addition to device/inode/type/size,
from the pre-prefix stat through the pre-full-read and post-full-read stats. Reject any change. Add a
regression that performs an equal-size in-place rewrite between prefix classification and the full
read and expects `absent`.

## LOW

None.

# Prior finding recheck

- R1 resolved: `scripts/kaola-workflow-adaptive-node.js:6501-6504,6842-6858` carries fresh/absent
  proof only into the immediate dispatch card and does not persist it in `running-set.json`.
  `node scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` passed all 2,169 assertions.
- R2 resolved for bounded discovery: `scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:51-56,92-168`
  bounds files, directories, depth, entries, prefix reads, and the retained rollout. Manual limit
  probes returned absent at depth, directory, and entry exhaustion.
- R3 resolved: malformed unrelated JSONL remains skippable, uniquely bound malformed JSONL returns
  absent, and an unclassifiable metadata-prefix remainder fails closed. Resolver tests passed.
- R4 resolved: `scripts/kaola-workflow-next-action.js:49-53,115-123` serializes only
  status/model/reasoning_effort; thread id, timestamp, and source remain local to proof loading.
- R5 resolved: the targeted static-pin wording scan is clean across schema, resolver, tests, and all
  three contract-validator editions. Remaining `codex_profile_tier_mismatch` occurrences are
  negative guards that prevent the retired token from returning.
- R6 resolved as scoped: `scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:128-173,194-195` opens with
  `O_RDONLY|O_NOFOLLOW|O_NONBLOCK`, retains that descriptor through bounded prefix/full parsing, and
  closes it in `finally`. A post-prefix pathname swap kept the original `high` proof; pre-open symlink
  and FIFO substitutions returned absent without blocking; a 16 MiB+1 candidate returned absent.
  R10 is the distinct same-inode/version-stability residual.
- R7 resolved: `scanComplete` now records file, directory, depth, entry, and metadata-prefix
  truncation. Exactly 2,048 JSONL files with EOF returned fresh; adding a 2,049th matching duplicate
  returned absent. The committed resolver suite covers exact-bound EOF, file-frontier truncation,
  matching duplicates beyond the frontier, and prefix-bound ambiguity.
- R8 resolved: `scripts/kaola-workflow-codex-preflight.js:845-848,1066-1072` treats an exact historical
  pair as stale only when the remaining validation reasons are exactly the two runtime-key omission
  reasons. A legacy pair plus `name="wrong-role"` produced zero legacy entries and one malformed entry
  with the role-name diagnostic.
- R9 resolved: profile/runtime comments now describe parent-session inheritance, declarative tier
  metadata, or historical migration pairs rather than active Sol/medium and Sol/xhigh profile pins.

# Complete accumulated-diff review

- Scope is controlled: the frozen n4 write set contains 96 paths; the accumulated product diff has
  91 tracked paths and `outside=[]`. The five allowed-but-unchanged paths are the three plan-run
  command surfaces and the two forge workflow-script tests. No public docs, changelog, opencode
  configuration, or unrelated product files changed.
- All 48 role profiles omit executable `model` and `model_reasoning_effort` keys. The mechanical audit
  found 16 roles, zero pins, and zero cross-forge profile drift.
- Fresh parent proof gives both reasoning and standard metadata tiers the same parent pair; absent
  proof gives explicit null inherited fields. Known roles report `codex_profile_mode:"inherit"` and
  remain compatible regardless of authored tier metadata.
- The reasoning floor remains fail-closed: missing proof returns
  `reasoning_floor_proof_missing`, `high` returns `reasoning_floor_violation`, and a fresh current
  `gpt-5.6-sol/xhigh` proof passes. Claude/opencode tier mappings and tier-derived wait budgets remain
  covered by the edition suites.

reasoning-floor proof can be fresh.

### R13 ‚Äî A pre-open file-type race can hide a duplicate rollout

`scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:123-140` enters the candidate branch only after
`Dirent` classified a regular `.jsonl`, but after opening and BigInt-fstatting the path, line 139 does
`if (!stat.isFile()) continue`. If the enumerated regular file is replaced by a directory or FIFO
before `openSync`, the nonblocking open may succeed, the non-file descriptor is silently ignored, and
`scanComplete` remains true. The byte-identical resolver copies under
`plugins/kaola-workflow{,-gitlab,-gitea}/scripts/kaola-workflow-resolve-agent-model.js:123-140` share
the behavior.

The review independently reproduced this with two same-thread regular rollouts. During `openSync` for
the second rollout, the harness renamed it aside, created a directory at its pathname, and let the
original open proceed. The loader returned the first rollout as `status:"fresh",
reasoning_effort:"high"`, even though the entry that had been classified as a possible duplicate
changed type before inspection completed. This violates the fail-closed unique-binding rule already
applied to open/fstat/read exceptions and descriptor lifecycle changes.

When a Dirent-classified regular JSONL opens to a non-regular descriptor, mark discovery incomplete
and refuse rather than continuing. Add a deterministic regular-to-directory or regular-to-FIFO race
regression beside the R11 candidate-I/O case at `scripts/test-agent-model-resolver.js:192-233`.

Every shipped Codex role profile inherits the current parent session model and reasoning effort; `reasoning` and `standard` remain declarative tier/wait-budget metadata; legacy or malformed installed pins cannot silently restore tier-selected runtime strength; the reasoning floor remains fail-closed.

## Counterexample search

- Remaining source pin: none. Mechanical search found zero top-level `model` or `model_reasoning_effort` keys across all 48 profiles.
- Edition drift: none. Same-role profile triples and the four resolver copies are byte-identical; edition sync passed all registered groups.
- Role-class runtime branch: none. Both tier classes produce null Codex pair values sourced from `parent_session`; only wait budgets differ (`reasoning=40`, `standard=20`).
- Transient override: none. The live n6 spawn input omitted both fields; routing reachability also pins omission for v1/v2 role dispatch.
- Candidate-profile substitution: none. n6 bound the child to the candidate `code-explorer` profile by exact symlink target/hash, role/nickname, and embedded unique developer instructions.
- Stale installed-profile acceptance: none. The negative matrix below refused every perturbation before repair.
- Card/prose restoration of static strength: none. Routing generation, required-token checks, negative phrase audits, and contract validators passed.
- Claude/opencode regression: none found. Their tier mappings remain independent of the Codex inheritance fields and the refreshed cross-edition receipt is green.

## Out-of-repository legacy/malformed profile matrix

Each case began from a freshly installed candidate profile, altered only the installed `implementer.toml`, ran read-only preflight, default autofix, and a second read-only preflight, then counted remaining pins.

| Perturbation | Initial refusal | Autofix | Second preflight | Remaining pins |
|--------------|-----------------|---------|------------------|----------------|
| model only | `profiles_malformed`, exit 1 | `status:ok`, `autofixed:true` | `status:ok`, `autofixed:false` | 0 |
| effort only | `profiles_malformed`, exit 1 | `status:ok`, `autofixed:true` | `status:ok`, `autofixed:false` | 0 |
| Sol/medium pair | `profiles_stale`, exit 1 | `status:ok`, `autofixed:true` | `status:ok`, `autofixed:false` | 0 |
| Sol/xhigh pair | `profiles_stale`, exit 1 | `status:ok`, `autofixed:true` | `status:ok`, `autofixed:false` | 0 |

The partial cases are deliberately classified more strictly as malformed while still reporting stale posture and offering the safe reinstall. Exact historical pairs take the migratable stale branch. All four converge idempotently on inherited omission without changing root-level user posture.

## Reasoning-floor falsification

| Proof posture | Result |
|---------------|--------|
| fresh current `gpt-5.6-sol/xhigh` | pass, floor `gpt-5.6-sol/xhigh` |
| fresh current `gpt-5.6-sol/high` | `reasoning_floor_violation` |
| absent proof | `reasoning_floor_proof_missing` |
| stale/wrong-thread proof | `reasoning_floor_proof_stale` |
| unknown model with `ultra` effort | `reasoning_floor_violation` |

Tier metadata alone therefore cannot satisfy the Codex reasoning floor, and a stronger-looking effort on an unclassified model is not guessed safe.
